channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Success Center Review Sessions: Week of 4/12-4/18
item.description: Week of 4/12- 4/18: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! Thermodynamics: Sunday, 4/12 (5-7pm) at the Success Center Essence of Eng. Mechanics: Monday, 4/13 (5-7pm) at the Success Center Computer Science: Tuesday, 4/14 (7-9pm) with SASE in the Nason Classroom Diff Eq.: Wednesday, 4/15 (7-9pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Mario Kart & Pizza Break with Lally
item.description: Take a break from classes and join students from across campus for an afternoon of Mario Kart, pizza, popcorn, and Lally swag in PITTS 5200. Whether you’re racing to win or just here for the pizza, it’s the perfect way to recharge and unwind as the semester wraps up. Wednesday, April 15 | 11:30 a.m.–2:00 p.m. No sign-up needed, just drop in and play!
item.author: Students

item.title: Attention: First-Year Students & Graduating Seniors
item.description: Help Us Improve Your RPI Experience. Share Your Voice! Take the National Survey of Student Engagement (NSSE) survey to help RPI improve your educational experience. Check your email for your personal invitation to participate!
item.author: Students

item.title: McKinney Awards Ceremony - TODAY 4/9
item.description: TODAY: The 2026 Annual McKinney Reading Series and Student Awards Ceremony will take place on Thursday, April 9, 2026, from 7:00–9:00 p.m. at the Heffner Alumni House. The event will feature Hisham Matar, the Pulitzer Prize–winning author of three novels and two memoirs. The evening will include the announcement of the McKinney Student Writing Contest winners, a literary reading, and an audience Q&A, followed by a book signing. Books will be available for purchase at the event.
item.author: Community

item.title: Nominate a Graduate Student for Student Spotlight!
item.description: Know a graduate student who is doing fascinating work or has an interesting story to tell? We’d like to highlight their accomplishments on our Student Spotlight page. Who embodies the BEST in your department? Who presents innovative and exciting ideas? Who leads others to become their best selves? Faculty, students, and staff may nominate a student here: https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_1z9O3WGwx7ybE7c (Nominate a 2nd student by clicking the link again.) Questions? email madige@rpi.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Security - How to Recognize and Report Business Email Compromise (BEC) Emails
item.description: Business Email Compromise (BEC) is a sophisticated cybercrime targeting Organizations and Universities where attackers impersonate trusted individuals including Executives, Faculty or Staff, and trusted vendors. Through impersonation, these bad actors will attempt to trick victims into transferring funds, revealing sensitive data, and other social engineering tactics attempting to extract personally identifiable information (PII).
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselyrics Night Live
item.description: Date: Saturday, April 11, 2026 8:00 PM - 10:00 PM - Join the Rensselyrics for their Spring Invitational, Rensselyrics Night Live, featuring The Skidmore Dynamics and UMass’ Duly Noted. Tickets will be sold 4/6-4/10 in the Union Lobby and DCC for $5 pre-sale, and available at the door for $7.
item.author: Community

item.title: NEW at CCPD: The Career Closet Open House!
item.description: Come check out the NEW CCPD Career Closet and see how we’re helping students show up with confidence! 🗓 Thursday, April 9 | ⏰ 11 a.m.–3 p.m. 📍 DCC, Great Hall 👗 Take a tour of the Career Closet (DCC 121) and learn about the new “My Career Closet” program. 🎁 BONUS: The f̳i̳r̳s̳t̳ ̳5̳0̳ ̳s̳t̳u̳d̳e̳n̳t̳s̳ to use the digital My Career Closet receive a FREE voucher! Don’t miss out on this exciting new CCPD resource—come see what the Career Closet can do for you!
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Student Film Festival
item.description: The RPI 2026 Student Film Festival is UPAC Cinema and the Arts Department’s fifth annual celebration of film at RPI. On Friday, April 10, 2026 we will be showcasing student work in DCC 308. After the reel of short films an award ceremony will take place. Best Picture, Jury Special, Best Cinematography, Best Audio, Best Editing, Best Acting, and Best Screenplay are the awarded categories this year. Join us from 7pm-9:30pm.
item.author: Community

item.title: Fireside Chat with CJ Winslow M.Eng ’06, B.S. ’05
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 9, 2026 5:00 PM - 7:00 PM - Design it. Build it. Grow it. Discover how CJ Winslow turned engineering, innovation, and manufacturing into a thriving company. Learn how technical skills, product development, and smart risk-taking can transform an engineer into a business leader. RSVP by 04/10/26 RSVP https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1USf7r28Dpbs63x_jYcjh_bG8qZh1X9fZ1vn-uH-ZSsk/edit?usp=sharing 4/13/26 5:00pm - 7:00pm in the Union Theater
item.author: Community

item.title: ISSS Hosts Free Immigration Workshop by NPZ Law Group
item.description: ISSS will host a virtual workshop presented by NPZ Group (Nachman, Phulwani, Zimovcak (NPZ) Law Group, P.C.). Hear from expert immigration lawyers about the visa options after graduation, including H-1B, O-1, and much more! Online event on Tuesday, April 21, 2026 from 2:00 p.m. - 3:30 p.m. Register at: https://tinyurl.com/NPZ-SP26-immigration
item.author: Community

item.title: Intellectual Property & Technology Licensing Seminar
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 11:00 AM - 1:00 PM - Are you working on groundbreaking research? Curious about how to protect, commercialize, or launch a start-up based on your innovations through IP? Join us for an exciting campuswide seminar hosted by the Office of Intellectual Property and Technology Licensing (IPTL) at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI)! This seminar is your chance to learn how IPTL supports faculty and students in translating research into real-world impact through…
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty Quick Learn Webinar: Tips for Planning Your Condensed Course
item.description: Date: Monday, April 13, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - The Planning Your Condensed Course webinar is meant to assist instructors teaching in The Arch or short semester courses in planning and presenting their course material in innovative, effective, and carefully considered ways. In this webinar we will offer quick tips on best practices in condensing coursework. Please register using the Event URL in the See section of the calendar event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop: “Leading from Anywhere” April 30, 2026 - 8:30 AM - 11:00 AM
item.description:
At Rensselaer, we maintain that “leadership” involves a learnable set of actions and behaviors that is available to everyone. Whether it be leading a particular project or initiative, assisting others in overcoming obstacles and capitalizing on opportunities, or catalyzing co-creation with colleagues, Rensselaer community members model leadership every day. In this workshop, participants will explore the tenants that comprise everyday leadership and discuss the behaviors that define leadership…

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
